*From the Earth to the Moon* is Jules Verne's satirical space-adventure, in which the [[Gun Club]] of [[baltimore|Baltimore]] turns its ballistic genius toward the Moon. It opens with the club's founding — "During the Federal war in the United States a new and very influential club was established in the city of [[baltimore|Baltimore]], Maryland" (FEM-001-002).

The telescope of the Rocky Mountains "brings the moon to within two leagues only" and "allows them to see objects having nine feet of diameter on her surface." (RM-020-037) The heights are prone to obscuring cloud, "the great tempest which swept away the clouds heaped up on the Rocky Mountains." (RM-020-019) The range is also a barrier to travel: in *Around the World in Eighty Days*, Fogg's train reaches "Bridger Pass, seven thousand five hundred and twenty-four feet above the level of the sea, one of the highest points attained by the track in crossing the Rocky Mountains."
